revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Iwai-jinja (Tokyo)]][[ja:磐井神社]] {{Expand Japanese|磐井神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Iwai Shrine | native_name = 磐井神社 | image = Iwai-jinja (Tokyo), haiden-1.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Iwai Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|35.585157|139.735322}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|lt=Ōjin|ar|الإمبراطور أوجين|arz|الامبراطور اوجين|azb|اوجین (ایمپیراتور)|bn|সম্রাট ওজিন|cs|Ódžin|de|Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|es|Ōjin Tennō|eu|Ōjin enperadorea|fa|امپراتور اوجین|fi|Ōjin|fr|Ōjin|hr|Ōjin|id|Kaisar Ōjin|it|Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|ka|ოძინი|ko|오진 천황|nl|Ojin|pl|Ōjin|pt|Ojin|ru|Император Одзин|sh|Car Ōjin|simple|Emperor Ōjin|sl|Cesar Odžin|sv|Ojin|th|จักรพรรดิโอจิง|tl|Emperador Ōjin|tr|Ōjin (imparator)|uk|Імператор Одзін|uz|Imperator Ōjin|vi|Thiên hoàng Ōjin|yo|Emperor Ōjin|zh_min_nan|Ôzin Thian-hông|zh_yue|應神天皇|zh|应神天皇|qid=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} }} {{nihongo|'''Iwai Shrine'''|磐井神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Musashi Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Q134917286|ltq=Q134917286|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|en|Shikinai Shosha|ja|式内小社|simple|Shikinai Shosha|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Q11390939|ltq=Q11390939|qid=Q11390939|1=Hachiman shrine}} * {{ill|Q134987079|ltq=Q134987079|qid=Q134987079|1=shrine dedicated to Empress Jingū}} * {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|lt=Kokuhei-sha|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Hiteisha|lt=Shikinai Hiteisha|ja|比定社|qid=Q135026601|1=Shikinai Hiteisha}} == part of entity (P131) == * {{ill|Q217234|ltq=Q217234|qid=Q217234|1=Ōta, Tokyo}} * {{ill|Musashi Province|lt=Musashi Province|ar|محافظة موساشي|ca|Província de Musashi|ckb|پارێزگای موساشی|cs|Provincie Musaši|de|Provinz Musashi|en|Musashi Province|eo|Provinco Musaŝi|es|Provincia de Musashi|fa|ولایت موساشی|fr|Province de Musashi|gl|Provincia de Musashi|hak|Musashi-koet|id|Provinsi Musashi|it|Musashi (provincia)|ja|武蔵国|ko|무사시국|lt|Musašio žemė|nl|Musashi (provincie)|pl|Prowincja Musashi|pt|Província de Musashi|ru|Мусаси (провинция)|simple|Musashi Province|sk|Musaši (provincia)|sr|Провинција Мусаши|th|แคว้นมูซาชิ|tr|Musashi (vilayet)|uk|Провінція Мусаші|vi|Musashi (tỉnh)|zh_min_nan|Musasi-kok|zh_yue|武藏國|zh|武藏國|qid=Q907398|1=Musashi Province}} * {{ill|Q1278470|ltq=Q1278470|qid=Q1278470|1=Ebara district}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|35.585157|139.735322}} == image (P18) == * Iwai-jinja (Tokyo), haiden-1.jpg == dedicated to (P825) == * {{ill|Q317997|ltq=Q317997|qid=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} * {{ill|Q261637|ltq=Q261637|qid=Q261637|1=Hachiman}} == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 181690 ** P3831: {{ill|Kokugakuin University Shrine database|lt=Kokugakuin University Shrine database|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Shinto Directive|lt=Shinto Directive|en|Shinto Directive|fa|بخشنامه شینتو|fr|Directive shinto|id|Pengarahan Shinto|ja|神道指令|zh|神道指令|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == name in kana (P1814) == * いわいじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5010805000098 == Commons category (P373) == * Iwai-jinja (Tokyo) == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '東京都大田区大森北2-20-8', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Iwai-jinja (Tokyo)}} [[JA:磐井神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11587853}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Iwai Shrine |画像=[[File:Iwai-jinja (Tokyo), haiden-1.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |所在地={{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|qid=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Ōta|Q217234|qid=Q217234|1=Ōta, Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Ōmori-kita|Q11436326|qid=Q11436326|1=Ōmori-Kita}}2-20-8 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|35|6.6|N|139|44|7.8|E|display=inline,title}} |創建=Unknown |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}{{sfn|Tokyo Metropolitan Association of Shinto Shrines}} |祭神={{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|qid=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}}
{{ill|lt=Emperor Chūai|Q179971|qid=Q179971|1=Emperor Chūai}}
{{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|qid=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hime Ōkami|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|qid=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}{{sfn|Tokyo Metropolitan Association of Shinto Shrines}} |本殿={{ill|lt=Hachiman-zukuri|Q3125455|qid=Q3125455|1=Hachiman-zukuri}} copper-sheet roof |札所等={{ill|lt=Tōkai Seven Lucky Gods|Q28685055|qid=Q28685055|1=Tōkai Seven Lucky Gods}} |例祭=From {{ill|lt=Friday|Q130|qid=Q130|1=Friday}} to {{ill|lt=Sunday|Q132|qid=Q132|1=Sunday}} in the first week of {{ill|lt=August|Q122|qid=Q122|1=August}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Tokyo city|lat_deg=35|lat_min=35|lat_sec=6.6|lon_deg=139|lon_min=44|lon_sec=7.8|label=Iwai Shrine|position=right|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|35|6.6|N|139|44|7.8|E}}}} }} [[File:Iwai-jinja (Tokyo), torii-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Iwai Shrine''' (いわいじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} ({{ill|lt=Hachimangū|Q11390939|qid=Q11390939|1=Hachiman shrine}}) in {{ill|lt=Ōmori-kita|Q11436326|qid=Q11436326|1=Ōmori-Kita}}, {{ill|lt=Ōta|Q217234|qid=Q217234|1=Ōta, Tokyo}}, {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|qid=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} its rank was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. Before the Edo period, it was also called '''Suzumori Hachimangū''' (Suzunomori Hachimangū / Suzugamori Hachimangū). It is also said to have been positioned as the "general Hachiman shrine of Musashi Province." In the precincts, {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q818468|lt=Benzaiten|1=Benzaiten}} (Kasajima Benten Shrine), one of the "Tōkai Seven Lucky Gods," is enshrined. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|qid=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} * {{ill|lt=Emperor Chūai|Q179971|qid=Q179971|1=Emperor Chūai}} * {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|qid=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hime Ōkami|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|qid=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}} === History === Its founding date is unknown, but it is traditionally said to have been founded in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Bidatsu|Q316637|qid=Q316637|1=Emperor Bidatsu}}{{sfn|Tokyo Metropolitan Association of Shinto Shrines}}. According to the {{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|qid=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}, in {{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|qid=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 1 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|qid=Q23897|1=859}}) it is recorded that "Iwai Shrine of Musashi Province, Junior Fifth Rank, was listed as an official shrine." In the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|qid=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, it was also listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|qid=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}. It is also said to have been designated the general Hachiman shrine ({{ill|lt=Kokufu Hachimangū|Q11420856|qid=Q11420856|lt=Sōsha Hachimangū|1=Kokubu-Hachimangu}}) of {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|qid=Q907398|1=Musashi Province}}. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|qid=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it received the devotion of the shogunal house and was also called "Suzugamori Hachiman(gū)"{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō, Irido Village}}. The place name Suzugamori is said to come from the "bell stone" passed down at this shrine (a stone that rings like a {{ill|lt=bell|Q7651136|qid=Q7651136|1=suzu}}). === Precincts === File:Iwai-jinja (Tokyo), shaden.jpg|Shrine buildings File:Iwai-jinja (Tokyo), honden.jpg|Honden File:Iwai-jinja (Tokyo), sekihigun.jpg|Stone monument group File:Iwai-jinja (Tokyo), kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall and Kaihō Inari Shrine File:Iwai-jinja (Tokyo), iwai.jpg|Iwai Well (inscription by former {{ill|lt=viscount|Q185902|qid=Q185902|1=Viscount}} {{ill|lt=Tachibana Tanetada|Q50641080|qid=Q50641080|1=Tachibana Tanetada}}) === Cultural properties === === Ōta City designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural property ** Bell stone, crow stone, and Edo literati stone monument group (epigraphy) - bell stone and crow stone are not open to the public. Designated February 2, 1974 (Shōwa 49){{PDFlink|[https://web.archive.org/web/20220109013831/https://www.city.ota.tokyo.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai_hogo/shitei_torokubunkazai/bunkazai_list.files/20190401bunka_ku_shitei.pdf Ōta City designated cultural properties list]}} (Ōta City website).. * Historic sites ** Iwai Shrine - designated February 2, 1974 (Shōwa 49). ** Iwai Well - designated February 2, 1974 (Shōwa 49). === Access === * {{ill|lt=Keikyu Corporation|Q1322206|qid=Q1322206|1=Keikyu}}{{ill|lt=Ōmori-kaigan Station|Q1592872|qid=Q1592872|1=Ōmorikaigan Station}} === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * {{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/ota/5460/|title=Iwai Shrine|accessdate=2020-07-13|ref=harv|publisher= Tokyo Metropolitan Association of Shinto Shrines}} * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|lt=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|Q11503943|qid=Q11503943|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō}}|volume=Vol. 43 Ebara District No. 5|chapter=Irido Village Suzumori Hachiman Shrine|id={{NDLJP|763981/43}}|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Home Affairs|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Irido Village}}}} === Related items === *{{ill|lt=Suzugamori Execution Ground|Q11648348|qid=Q11648348|1=Suzugamori execution grounds}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Iwai-jinja (Tokyo)}} *[https://iwaijinja.tokyo/ Iwai Shrine] - Official website {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Iwai Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Iwai Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ota, Tokyo]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tokyo]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Tokyo Metropolis]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Empress Jingū]]
